# Code of Conduct

Metarc is a project dedicated to technical exploration and efficiency. To keep our community productive and welcoming, we follow a simple set of principles:

### 1. Focus on the code, not the person
Critique ideas, implementation, and performance—not the individual. We value rigorous technical debate, but it must always stay professional. A better algorithm is the goal; winning an argument is not.

### 2. Be excellent to each other
Treat fellow contributors with respect. We are all here to learn and build something interesting. Assume positive intent, even when technical disagreements arise.

### 3. High signal, low noise
Keep discussions constructive. If you find a flaw, try to suggest a path forward. Avoid toxic behavior, harassment, or exclusionary language. We prioritize a healthy environment over "rockstar" egos.

### 4. Assume good faith
Open source communication (especially async) can be tricky. If a comment seems blunt, assume it was meant to be efficient, not offensive. If you're the one writing, try to be direct but kind.
